title: Multi-Project Contributors
description: You are given four tables: departments, employees, projects, and project assignments. Find names of employees who are assigned to at least 2 projects with a budget greater than 100000, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
departments(id, name, location)
employees(id, name, dept_id)
projects(id, title, budget)
project_assignments(id, employee_id, project_id, role)
outputFormat: One employee name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use three JOINs and GROUP BY. Filter WHERE budget > 100000. Use HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN project_assignments pa ON e.id = pa.employee_id JOIN projects p ON pa.project_id = p.id WHERE p.budget > 100000 GROUP BY e.id, e.name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY e.name;
hint: Join employees → project_assignments → projects, filter budget > 100000, GROUP BY employee, HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT, location TEXT); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER, title TEXT, budget INTEGER); CREATE TABLE project_assignments (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Engineering','NYC'),(2,'Research','LA'); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Alice',1),(2,'Bob',1),(3,'Carol',2); INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1,'ProjectA',150000),(2,'ProjectB',80000),(3,'ProjectC',200000); INSERT INTO project_assignments VALUES (1,1,1,'dev'),(2,1,3,'lead'),(3,2,1,'dev'),(4,2,2,'dev'),(5,3,3,'dev');

CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT, location TEXT); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER, title TEXT, budget INTEGER); CREATE TABLE project_assignments (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'IT','Boston'),(2,'Finance','Chicago'); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Dan',1),(2,'Eve',1),(3,'Frank',2),(4,'Grace',2); INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1,'Alpha',120000),(2,'Beta',200000),(3,'Gamma',50000),(4,'Delta',300000); INSERT INTO project_assignments VALUES (1,1,1,'dev'),(2,1,2,'dev'),(3,1,4,'lead'),(4,2,1,'dev'),(5,2,3,'dev'),(6,3,2,'lead'),(7,3,4,'dev'),(8,4,3,'dev');

CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT, location TEXT); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER, title TEXT, budget INTEGER); CREATE TABLE project_assignments (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Marketing','Miami'); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Hank',1),(2,'Iris',1); INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1,'CampA',90000),(2,'CampB',95000); INSERT INTO project_assignments VALUES (1,1,1,'lead'),(2,1,2,'lead'),(3,2,1,'dev');

CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT, location TEXT); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER, title TEXT, budget INTEGER); CREATE TABLE project_assignments (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Dev','Seattle'); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Jack',1),(2,'Kate',1),(3,'Leo',1); INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1,'P1',200000),(2,'P2',300000),(3,'P3',150000); INSERT INTO project_assignments VALUES (1,1,1,'lead'),(2,1,2,'dev'),(3,2,2,'lead'),(4,2,3,'dev'),(5,3,1,'dev'),(6,3,3,'lead');

CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER, name TEXT, location TEXT); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER, title TEXT, budget INTEGER); CREATE TABLE project_assignments (id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_id INTEGER, role TEXT); INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Ops','Denver'); INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Mia',1),(2,'Nan',1); INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1,'OpA',110000),(2,'OpB',120000),(3,'OpC',50000); INSERT INTO project_assignments VALUES (1,1,1,'lead'),(2,1,2,'dev'),(3,2,3,'dev');
